calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-26-2018 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-28-2018 Continued To Nov-14-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO STRIKE 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-21-2018 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Evan Krow, Esq. of Arnold & Porter, 650.319.4712, appearing for the Plaintiff;
John Vannucci, Esq. of the Law Office of John Vannucci, 415.981.7500, appearing for the Plaintiff;
Michael D. Mandell, Esq. of Belzer & Murray LLP, 925.283.9977, appearing for the Defendant.

No argument is held. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant Saul Gevertz's motion to strike portions of the third amended complaint filed by plaintiff Evan Krow is off calendar as moot in light of the ruling on Mr. Gevertz's demurrer.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600, 415.551.5868, Email: torreanom@aol.com. =(302/HEK)

DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-21-2018 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
James A. DiBoise, Esq. of Arnold & Porter, 650.319.4712, appearing for the Plaintiff Evan Krow;
John Vannucci, Esq. of the Law Office of John Vannucci, 415.981.7500, appearing for the Plaintiff Evan Krow;
Michael D. Mandell, Esq. of Belzer & Murray LLP, 925.283.9977, appearing for the Defendant Saul Gevertz.

Ruling: ARGUED. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant Saul Gevertz's demurrer to all five causes of action alleged in the third amended complaint filed by plaintiff Evan Krow is sustained without leave to amend as to all five causes of action. The third amended complaint fails to allege a valid claim against Mr. Gevertz for either direct or alter ego liability. The allegations in the third amended complaint are not substantively different from the allegations in the second amended complaint. The addition of the conspiracy allegations does not create liability. "When a corporate employee acts in the course of his or her employment, on behalf of the corporation, there is no entity apart from the employee with whom the employee can conspire. [I]t is basic in the law of conspiracy that you must have two persons or entities to have a conspiracy. A corporation cannot conspire with itself any more than a private individual can, and it is the general rule that the acts of the agent are the acts of the corporation...." (Black v. Bank of America (1994) 30 Cal.App.4th 1, 6 (internal quotations omitted).) Order signed. (see order for full details)

Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600, 415.551.5868, Email: torreanom@aol.com. =(302/HEK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-10-2018 Continued To Aug-01-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-25-2018 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, John F. Vannucci of the law offices of John F. Vannucci (415-981-7500) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Michael D. Mandell of the law offices of Belzer & Murray LLP (925-283-9977) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

The cause is argued within the context of a dmurrer, also on calendar this date, and issubmitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

DEFENDANT SAUL GEVERTZ'S MOTION TO STRIKE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT is off calendar in light of the ruling of Mr. Gevertz's demurrer to the second amended complaint, which was sustained with leave to amend.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, e-mail: torreanom@aol.org (L&M302)

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-25-2018 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, John F. Vannucci of the law offices of John F. Vannucci (415-981-7500) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Michael D. Mandell of the law offices of Belzer & Murray LLP (925-283-9977) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling with modification.

DEFENDANT SAUL GEVERTZ'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT, as to all five causes of action in the second amended complaint filed by plaintiff Evan Krow is SUSTAINED WITH FORTY days leave to amend to all five causes of action. All five causes of action fail based on either direct liability or alter ego liability for the conduct of Fox Ortega Enterprises due to insufficient allegations of required elements. Direct liability for the breach of contract and common counts claims fails due to the absence of any allegation that Mr. Gevertz was a contracting party. Direct liability on the misrepresentation claims fails due to the absence of any allegation that Mr. Gevertz personally made any misrepresentation that was relied on by Mr. Krow. Direct liability on the negligence claim fails due to the absence of any allegations of facts showing that Mr. Gevertz owed a duty sounding in negligence law to Mr. Krow. Alter ego liability fails due to the absence of sufficient allegations showing that there was a unity of interest between Mr. Gevertz and Fox Ortega Enterprises such that in reality they were one and the same. If he can do so in good faith, Mr. Krow is given one last opportunity to correct the deficiencies in his claims and, if he chooses to do so, Mr. Krow must allege facts showing that the claims are not time-barred.

Written order is signed and filed.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, e-mail: torreanom@aol.org (L&M302)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-09-2018 Continued To May-30-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-30-2018 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling. DEFENDANT SAUL GEVERTZ'S MOTION TO STRIKE [the punitive damages and emotional distress allegations in the] 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is off calendar in light of the ruling on Mr. Gevertz's demurrer to the first amended complaint. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR#8600, email: torreanom@aol.com; mtorreano@sftc.org.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-30-2018 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance.

RULING: There being no appearance or objection, the Court adopts the tentative ruling on DEFENDANT SAUL GEVERTZ'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT as follows:

Defendant Saul Gevertz's demurrer to all five causes of action in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Evan Krow is sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to all five causes of action. The complaint is uncertain and/or fails to allege sufficient ultimate facts as to each of the five claims. As to the claims for breach of contract and common counts, it is not clear whether Mr. Krow alleges that he entered into one or more contracts with Mr. Gevertz, as opposed to entering into a contract with an entity such as Fox Ortega Enterprises for which Mr. Gevertz may have worked as an agent and/or an employee. If Mr. Krow alleges that he entered into one or more contracts with Mr. Gevertz, he must either attach those contracts to his second amended complaint or plead the legal effect of the contracts including the dates they were entered into and their material terms. If Mr. Krow does not allege that he entered into a contract with Mr. Gevertz directly, he must allege why he believes that Mr. Gevertz is liable for breach of contract and common counts notwithstanding that Mr. Gevertz is not a party to a contract with Mr. Krow. As to the claims for intentional and negligent misrepresentations, it is not clear whether Mr. Krow alleges that Mr. Gevertz made any of the alleged misrepresentations. If Mr. Krow alleges that Mr. Gevertz made the alleged misrepresentations, Mr. Krow must allege when Mr Gevertz made the representations and to whom. If Mr. Krow does not allege that Mr. Gevertz made the alleged misrepresentations, he must allege why he believes that Mr. Gevertz is liable for the misrepresentations notwithstanding that Mr. Gevertz did not make the misrepresentations. As to the claim for negligence, it is not clear whether Mr. Krow alleges that Mr. Gevertz committed any negligent conduct. If Mr. Krow alleges that Mr. Gevertz committed one or more negligent acts, he must allege what those acts were and when they were committed. If Mr. Krow does not allege that Mr. Gevertz committed any negligent acts, he must allege why he believes that Mr. Gevertz is liable for the negligence committed by someone else. If liability against Mr. Gevertz is based on the alter ego doctrine, Mr. Krow must allege ultimate facts showing the elements of that doctrine, including control of the corporation and need to avoid an inequitable result, which he has not done.

[ORDER SIGNED AFTER HEARING.] Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR#8600, email: torreanom@aol.com; mtorreano@sftc.org.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-28-2017 Continued To Feb-28-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

RULING: There being no appearance or objection, the Court adopts the tentative ruling on DEFENDANT SAUL GEVERTZ'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT as follows: Plaintiff Evan Krow is granted permission to file a first amended complaint, which moots the demurrer. Counsel for Mr. Krow is to submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and complies with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Denise Doucette, CSR#5963, Tel. 707.246.3323.

****After hearing, the Court signed one ORDER for both Demurrer and Motion To Strike.****
